statement.p: By your cell phone contract, you pay a monthly fee plus {\$0.07} for each minute you spend on the phone. In one month, you spent 240 minutes over the phone, and had a bill totaling {\$32.80}.

Let x be the number of minutes you spend on the phone in a month, and let y be your total cell phone bill for that month, in dollars. Use a linear equation to model your monthly bill based on the number of minutes you spend on the phone.
This lines slope-intercept equation is .
If you spend 110 minutes on the phone in a month, you would be billed .
If your bill was {\$48.90} one month, you must have spent minutes on the phone in that month.

li.p: A lines equation in slope-intercept form looks like y=mx+b, where m is the slope, and y is the y-coordinate of the y-intercept. The slope of the linear model has been given to us: {\$0.07} per minute.
So now we have that y=0.07x+b. The next step is to find the value of b. There are at least two ways.
One way is to substitute a given point into y={0.07x}+b. We have the point (240,32.8).

p.m: \begin{aligned} y \amp = {0.07x} + b \\ 32.8 \amp = 0.07 \cdot 240 + b \\ 32.8 \amp = 16.8 + b \\ 32.8\mathbf{{}-16.8} \amp = 16.8 + b\mathbf{{}-16.8} \\ 16 \amp = b\\ b \amp = 16 \end{aligned}

li.p: This lines slope-intercept equation is y={0.07x}+16.
Another way is to use the lines point-slope equation y-y_{1}=m(x-x_{1}). Again, we use the point (240,32.8).
This is the second way to find b, and thus the lines slope-intercept equation is y=0.07x+16.
In one month, you spent 110 minutes on the phone. To find out the bill, we substitute 110 in for x in the equation y=0.07x+16, and we have:
If you spent 110 minutes over the phone in a month, your bill for that month would be {\$23.70}.
In one month, you had a cell phone bill of {\$48.90}. To find how many minutes you spent on the phone that month, we substitute 48.9 in for y in the equation y=0.07x+16, and we have:
If your bill was {\$48.90} in one month, you must have spent 470 minutes on the phone.
